bills.title: Generative AI Framework for Public Education - Privacy, AI literacy, professional development
bills.ai_summary: Idaho SB1227 establishes a generative AI framework for public education focusing on student privacy protection, AI literacy curriculum, and teacher professional development in AI use. The bill is now law after Governor Little's signature on April 26, 2026.

bills.title: Worker Protections from AI Use in the workplace
bills.ai_summary: California SB947 would protect workers from potentially harmful AI use in employment decisions and workplace monitoring by establishing worker protections and disclosure requirements. The bill is currently in Labor Committee hearing as of April 8, 2026.

bills.title: Health Carrier AI Clinical Judgment regulation
bills.ai_summary: New Hampshire HB1406 would regulate health insurance carriers' use of AI in clinical judgment decisions, likely requiring transparency or limitations on automated coverage denials. The bill is currently in Senate Commerce hearing as of April 7, 2026.

bills.title: Deepfake Extortion and Threats criminalization
bills.ai_summary: California SB1015 criminalizes the creation and use of deepfakes for extortion, harassment, or threats, adding penalties for non-consensual synthetic media used to harm others. The bill is currently in Public Safety Committee hearing as of April 7, 2026.

bills.title: Advertisement AI Disclosure requirements
bills.ai_summary: California SB1050 would require AI-generated advertisements to include clear disclosures notifying consumers that the ad was created using artificial intelligence. The bill is currently in Privacy Committee hearing as of April 6, 2026.

bills.title: Chatbot Toys Prohibition
bills.ai_summary: California SB867 would prohibit the sale and distribution of AI-powered chatbot toys designed to simulate human interaction with children. The bill is currently in Privacy Committee hearing as of April 6, 2026.

bills.title: Chatbot Safety for Minors
bills.ai_summary: Colorado HB1263 establishes safety requirements and protections for minors interacting with chatbots, likely including age verification and content guardrails. The bill is currently at House floor hearing as of April 2, 2026.

bills.title: Chatbot Age Verification and Harm Prevention
bills.ai_summary: Missouri HB2032 requires chatbots to implement age verification mechanisms and incorporate harm prevention features to protect minors from dangerous content. The bill passed its HCS committee 9-0 on April 2, 2026.

bills.title: Chatbot Disclosure and Child Safety Act
bills.ai_summary: Georgia SB540 requires chatbots to disclose they are AI and implement child safety protections including content filtering and parental controls. The bill is on the Governor's desk as of April 1, 2026.

bills.title: Insurance Healthcare AI Prohibition
bills.ai_summary: Georgia SB444 would prohibit health insurance companies from using AI in coverage decisions and medical treatment denials, restricting algorithmic healthcare rationing. The bill is on the Governor's desk as of April 1, 2026.

bills.title: Deepfakes in Political Materials regulation
bills.ai_summary: Maryland SB141 regulates the creation and distribution of deepfakes in political materials, requiring disclosures and establishing penalties for deceptive AI-generated campaign content. The bill passed the Senate 44-0 and is currently in House hearing as of April 1, 2026.

bills.title: AI Impact Study Committee creation
bills.ai_summary: Georgia SR789 creates a legislative study committee to examine AI's impact on Georgia's economy, workforce, and regulatory environment. The resolution passed the Senate on March 31, 2026.

bills.title: Data centers; site assessment, sound profile on residential units and schools within 500 feet
bills.ai_summary: Virginia HB153 requires data centers to conduct sound impact assessments for residential units and schools within 500 feet, addressing noise pollution from AI infrastructure. The bill is enrolled as of March 30, 2026.

bills.title: Certain data from water users; water use consumption reporting for data centers
bills.ai_summary: Virginia HB496 mandates that data centers report water consumption data to enable public monitoring of resource use by AI and computing facilities. The bill is enrolled as of March 30, 2026.

bills.title: Conversational AI Safety Act
bills.ai_summary: Idaho SB1297 establishes safety and transparency requirements for conversational AI systems, likely including safeguards against harmful outputs and user disclosure of AI interaction. The bill is on the Governor's desk as of March 28, 2026.

bills.title: Stop Harms from Addictive Social Media (AI-related child safety)
bills.ai_summary: Idaho HB542 addresses AI-driven child safety by restricting addictive social media design features that disproportionately affect minors through algorithmic engagement. The bill is on the Governor's desk as of March 28, 2026.

bills.title: AI Public Safety and Child Protection Transparency Act
bills.ai_summary: Illinois SB3261 requires AI systems used in public safety and child protection to maintain transparency in their operations and algorithmic decision-making. The bill is past the AI Committee deadline as of March 27, 2026.

bills.title: Chatbot Provider Liability Act
bills.ai_summary: Illinois HB5044 establishes liability standards for chatbot providers when their systems cause harm, enabling injured parties to pursue legal action against developers. The bill is currently in Judiciary Committee hearing as of March 26, 2026.

bills.title: Intimate Digital Depictions prohibition
bills.ai_summary: Missouri HB1913 criminalizes the creation and distribution of non-consensual intimate images created or manipulated using AI, including deepfake pornography. The bill passed its HCS committee 10-0 on March 26, 2026.

bills.title: AI Use in Education Commission
bills.ai_summary: Illinois HB5113 creates a commission to study and develop recommendations for AI use in K-12 schools, addressing curriculum integration and student impacts. The bill is currently in Education Committee hearing as of March 25, 2026.

bills.title: Synthetic Media Video Voyeurism criminalisation
bills.ai_summary: Idaho HB727 criminalizes the creation and distribution of synthetic media depicting non-consensual intimate images or voyeuristic videos without consent. The bill is now law after Governor signature on March 25, 2026.

bills.title: Chatbot Therapy Prohibition
bills.ai_summary: Maryland HB883 prohibits chatbots from providing mental health therapy or diagnosis services, restricting AI use in clinical therapeutic settings. The bill passed the House 110-23 and is currently in Senate Finance as of March 25, 2026.

bills.title: Requires data centers to report monthly and yearly water consumption; prohibits water NDAs with political subdivisions
bills.ai_summary: Ohio HB784 requires data centers to publicly report monthly and yearly water consumption while prohibiting non-disclosure agreements that hide environmental impact data from local governments. The bill was referred to House Energy Committee as of March 24, 2026.

bills.title: Data Center Customer Protection Act of 2026
bills.ai_summary: Oklahoma HB2992 protects consumers and data center host communities by establishing transparency and customer protection standards for large data center operations. The bill passed the House (engrossed) as of March 24, 2026.

bills.title: AI Chatbot Safety - Safeguards for minors, suicidal ideation protocols
bills.ai_summary: Washington HB2225 requires AI chatbots to implement safety protocols for minors including content safeguards and crisis intervention for suicidal ideation. The bill is now law after Governor Ferguson's signature on March 24, 2026.

bills.title: AI Transparency - Watermarking and disclosure for AI-generated content
bills.ai_summary: Requires AI-generated content to include watermarks and clear disclosure labels to help consumers identify synthetic media. This law is now in effect after being signed by the governor.

bills.title: Generative AI Use Notification Requirement
bills.ai_summary: Mandates that businesses notify consumers when generative AI is used in decision-making or content creation that affects them. Currently in committee review at the consumer protection hearing stage.

bills.title: Deceptive Synthetic Media in Elections prohibition
bills.ai_summary: Prohibits the use of deceptive synthetic media (deepfakes) in election campaigns and materials to protect electoral integrity. The bill is undergoing final legislative drafting before potential passage.

bills.title: Responsible Technology in Schools Act - AI in education under educator supervision
bills.last_action: Full Senate approved 42-0, sent to House
bills.ai_summary: Requires schools to use AI in education only under direct educator supervision and establishes oversight standards for classroom AI tools. The bill passed the full Senate unanimously and is now being sent to the House for consideration.

bills.title: AI-Assisted Therapy Research Services regulation
bills.ai_summary: Establishes regulatory standards for AI systems used to provide therapy or mental health services, ensuring safety and efficacy. The bill is currently in the health subcommittee stage with further review needed.

bills.title: Kids Chatbot Safety Act
bills.ai_summary: Restricts AI chatbots targeting children by requiring age verification, parental consent, and prohibiting manipulative design practices. Senate leadership approved the bill and it is moving forward in the legislative process.

bills.title: Generative AI Provenance Data Requirement
bills.ai_summary: Requires generative AI systems to track and disclose provenance data showing the origin and training sources of generated content. The bill received house caucus approval and is advancing toward passage.

bills.title: AI Ethics Education in Schools
bills.ai_summary: Mandates that schools teach AI ethics and responsible AI use as part of the standard curriculum starting in K-12 education. Senate caucus approved the bill, moving it closer to final passage.

bills.title: AI Psychotherapy Services regulation
bills.ai_summary: Regulates the use of AI in psychotherapy services by requiring transparency, informed consent, and oversight by licensed mental health professionals. The bill passed its third reading in the House and is advancing toward final passage.

bills.title: Healthcare AI Use regulation
bills.ai_summary: Establishes regulations for healthcare AI systems including transparency requirements, bias auditing, and physician oversight in clinical decisions. The bill passed third reading in the House and is now moving forward.

bills.title: Kids Chatbot Safety Act
bills.ai_summary: Protects children from predatory AI chatbots by prohibiting targeted manipulation, requiring parental notification, and establishing safety standards. The bill is advancing with a positive recommendation for passage.

bills.title: Mental Health AI Prohibition
bills.ai_summary: Prohibits the use of AI systems to provide mental health treatment or therapy without licensed professional oversight. The bill received a favorable committee recommendation and is progressing through the legislature.

bills.title: AI-Generated Content Accountability Act
bills.ai_summary: Creates accountability requirements for AI-generated content including disclosure labels, liability protections, and enforcement mechanisms for misleading synthetic media. The bill passed committee review unanimously.

bills.title: Deepfake Digital Impersonation criminalization
bills.ai_summary: Criminalizes the creation and distribution of deepfakes used for sexual extortion, impersonation, or fraud with specific penalties. The bill passed committee review with unanimous support.

bills.title: Conversational AI Safety Act
bills.ai_summary: Establishes safety standards for conversational AI systems including transparency, bias testing, and content moderation requirements. The bill is attached to another legislative vehicle and is likely to pass.

bills.title: Unlawful AI-Generated Images criminalization
bills.ai_summary: Criminalizes the creation and distribution of non-consensual AI-generated intimate images and deepfake pornography. The House approved the bill overwhelmingly and it is now in Senate Judiciary committee review.

bills.title: AI in Political Campaigns regulation
bills.ai_summary: Requires disclosure and regulation of AI-generated political ads and campaign materials to prevent election manipulation and deception. The bill received committee approval and is moving forward.

bills.title: Sexual Extortion Penalties enhancement
bills.ai_summary: Enhances criminal penalties for sexual extortion and sextortion cases, including those involving AI-generated intimate images. The Senate approved the bill unanimously.

bills.title: Conversational AI Safety Act
bills.ai_summary: Establishes safety standards and transparency requirements for conversational AI systems to protect users from harmful content and manipulation. The bill is scheduled for consideration as unfinished business on the calendar.

bills.title: AI Healthcare and Insurance Utilization regulation
bills.ai_summary: Regulates the use of AI in healthcare and insurance decision-making including prior authorization and coverage determinations with transparency and appeal rights. Concurrence between chambers is pending for final passage.

bills.title: Stop Harm From Addictive Social Media Act (AI algorithmic protections)
bills.ai_summary: Addresses algorithmic harms from social media AI by restricting addictive design practices and requiring age-appropriate content protections for minors. The House passed it unanimously and the Senate is now reviewing it.

bills.title: Meaningful Human Control of AI in employment
bills.ai_summary: Requires meaningful human review and approval before AI systems make employment decisions including hiring, promotion, and termination. The bill is currently in committee review for further consideration.

bills.title: Automated Employment Decision Systems regulation
bills.ai_summary: Regulates automated employment decision systems (AI hiring tools) to ensure transparency, fairness testing, and employee rights to explanation and appeal. The bill is undergoing final legislative drafting.

bills.title: AI Deepfake Protections
bills.ai_summary: Creates protections against deepfakes and AI-generated sexual imagery including criminal penalties and civil remedies for victims. The Senate approved the bill unanimously.

bills.title: Creates the Data Center Study Commission
bills.ai_summary: Establishes a commission to study the economic and infrastructure impacts of data centers and AI computing on state resources and planning. The House passed the bill unanimously to create this study group.

bills.title: AI Systems Requirements and Policy Office - Companion chatbots, automated employment, synthetic content
bills.ai_summary: Establishes requirements for AI systems including companion chatbots, automated employment systems, and synthetic content generation, and creates a state policy office to oversee AI regulation. Currently with Legislative Commissioner as of March 17, 2026, indicating advanced legislative processing.

bills.title: Innovations and Responsible AI Use
bills.ai_summary: Promotes responsible AI innovation and use practices in Connecticut with broad application across sectors. Approved by committee with strong support (20-1 vote) on March 16, 2026, positioning it for further legislative advancement.

bills.title: Companion Chatbot Consumer Disclosure Warnings
bills.ai_summary: Requires disclosure warnings to consumers about companion chatbots' nature and limitations in New York. Currently in early stage (introduced March 15, 2026) with no further action yet.

bills.title: Consumer-Facing Interactive AI with Private Right of Action
bills.ai_summary: Grants consumers a private right of action to sue developers of consumer-facing interactive AI systems for violations in Washington state. Passed the legislature on March 15, 2026, moving toward executive consideration.

bills.title: Student privacy and digital operators - Prohibits using student data to train AI models
bills.ai_summary: Prohibits California schools and educational technology providers from using student data to train AI models, protecting student privacy. At Senate Rules Committee stage as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: Attorney AI Use Protections - Code of Civil Procedure amendments
bills.ai_summary: Amends California's Code of Civil Procedure to establish protections and disclosure requirements for attorney use of AI in legal practice. Currently in Appropriations Committee as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: Mental Health Professional AI Transcription regulation
bills.ai_summary: Regulates mental health professionals' use of AI transcription technology in California, ensuring appropriate safeguards for sensitive patient communications. At Senate Rules Committee stage as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: AI Disclosure and Provenance Data requirements
bills.ai_summary: Requires AI systems in California to disclose their use of AI and provide data about training sources and content provenance. At Privacy Committee stage as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: Digital Dignity Act - Deepfake protections
bills.ai_summary: Creates comprehensive deepfake protections in California by prohibiting non-consensual synthetic sexual content and establishing legal remedies for victims. Amended at Privacy Committee stage as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: Workplace Surveillance AI Disclosure requirements
bills.ai_summary: Requires employers in California to disclose use of surveillance AI in the workplace and provide employees notice of monitoring. At Privacy Committee stage as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: Chatbot Children's Safety requirements
bills.ai_summary: Establishes safety requirements for chatbots marketed to children in California, including protections against harmful content and inappropriate interactions. At Privacy Committee with amendments as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: Synthetic nudity and sexual depiction prohibition
bills.ai_summary: Prohibits creation and distribution of synthetic nudity and sexual depictions in Arizona without consent. Senate caucus approved the measure on March 15, 2026.

bills.title: Healthcare AI Coverage Regulation
bills.ai_summary: Regulates insurance coverage and payment decisions for healthcare AI systems in Alabama, ensuring appropriate review and authorization. At House calendar for second reading as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: AI Bill of Rights
bills.ai_summary: Establishes a Bill of Rights framework for AI use in Florida, protecting consumer rights and establishing accountability standards. Passed the Senate but declined by the House on March 15, 2026, likely stalling progress.

bills.title: Minor AI Companion Safeguards
bills.ai_summary: Implements safeguards for AI companion systems marketed to minors in Hawaii, protecting children from exploitation and harmful interactions. Senate committees approved on March 15, 2026.

bills.title: AI Operator Disclosures and Protocols
bills.ai_summary: Requires Hawaii AI operators to disclose their use of AI technology and establish clear operational protocols for consumer interactions. House committees approved on March 15, 2026.

bills.title: Data Center Accountability Act
bills.ai_summary: Establishes accountability standards for data centers in Colorado regarding environmental impact, resource use, and operational transparency. At Senate Transportation Committee as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: Data Center Development regulation
bills.ai_summary: Regulates development and operation of data centers in Colorado with focus on environmental and community considerations. At Energy Committee stage as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: AI Discriminatory Effect in Housing prohibition
bills.ai_summary: Prohibits AI systems from causing discriminatory effects in housing decisions and lending in Minnesota. At Senate Judiciary Committee as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: AI Disclosure Requirement for consumer interactions
bills.ai_summary: Requires companies in Minnesota to disclose when they use AI in consumer interactions and explain how AI affects decisions. At Commerce and Consumer Protection Committee as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: AI Medical Insurance Decisions regulation
bills.ai_summary: Regulates AI use in medical insurance decisions in Minnesota, requiring transparency and appeal mechanisms for AI-driven coverage denials. At Commerce and Consumer Protection Committee as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: Licensed Profession AI Services Prohibition
bills.ai_summary: Prohibits AI systems from providing services licensed to human professionals (attorneys, doctors, etc.) in New Hampshire. In House for consideration as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: AI Oversight Act - Attorney General Division creation
bills.ai_summary: Creates a new Attorney General division in New Hampshire dedicated to AI oversight and enforcement of AI regulations. At House Commerce and Consumer Affairs Committee as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: Healthcare Provider AI Use regulation
bills.ai_summary: Regulates healthcare providers' use of AI in Louisiana, establishing standards for appropriate integration and oversight of AI in clinical practice. At Health/Welfare Committee as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: AI Content Disclosure Requirement
bills.ai_summary: Requires disclosure to Louisiana consumers when AI-generated or AI-modified content is presented to them. At Commerce Committee as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: Kids Chatbot Safety Act
bills.ai_summary: Establishes safety requirements for AI chatbots targeting children, including content filtering and parental controls. Currently in Louisiana Commerce Committee as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: Louisiana Infrastructure AI Risk Protection Act
bills.ai_summary: Requires AI risk assessments for critical infrastructure systems and mandates security protocols to prevent AI-related failures. Currently in Louisiana Commerce Committee as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: Social Media Age Verification and Addictive Algorithms
bills.ai_summary: Requires age verification for social media platforms and restricts algorithmic feeds designed to be addictive to minors. Currently in Kentucky Senate Judiciary Committee as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: Mental Health Therapy Chatbots regulation
bills.ai_summary: Establishes licensing and oversight requirements for AI-powered mental health therapy chatbots to ensure clinical competency. Currently in Kentucky Senate Committee on Committees as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: AI Output Ownership and intellectual property
bills.ai_summary: Clarifies that creators retain ownership rights to outputs generated by AI tools they operate. Committee-approved and on Iowa calendar as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: AI in Election Materials and Interactions regulation
bills.ai_summary: Regulates the use of AI-generated content in election materials and voter interactions, requiring disclosure and accuracy standards. Passed Iowa House committee 18-3 as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: Algorithmic Pricing Regulation
bills.ai_summary: Prohibits algorithmic price discrimination and requires transparency when AI sets different prices for different consumers. Currently in Minnesota Commerce and Consumer Protection Committee as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: CSU Instructor Human Requirement (AI cannot replace instructors)
bills.ai_summary: Mandates that California State University maintain human instructors and prohibits AI from fully replacing classroom instruction. Currently in California Senate Rules Committee as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: Healthcare AI Use regulation
bills.ai_summary: Establishes regulations for AI use in healthcare settings, requiring transparency, validation, and physician oversight of diagnostic AI systems. Currently in California Labor and Employment Committee as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: CCPA AI Model Data Requests
bills.ai_summary: Allows consumers to request disclosure of personal data used to train AI models under California's privacy law (CCPA). Currently in California Privacy Committee as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: Youth Mental Health and AI Impact study
bills.ai_summary: Directs California to conduct a study on AI's impact on youth mental health and report findings to the legislature. Currently in California Senate Rules Committee as of March 15, 2026.

bills.title: Mental Health Professional AI Prohibition
bills.ai_summary: Prohibits AI systems from independently providing mental health services and requires licensed professionals to conduct all mental health therapy. Signed into law by Tennessee Governor on March 12, 2026.

bills.title: Right to Compute
bills.last_action: Adopted by House voice vote, sent to Senate
bills.ai_summary: Establishes a right for individuals to access and use computing resources without unreasonable interference or surveillance. Passed New Hampshire House by voice vote and sent to Senate as of March 11, 2026.

bills.title: Expedited approval and permitting procedures for AI data centers powered by small modular nuclear reactors
bills.ai_summary: Creates expedited permitting procedures for AI data centers powered by small modular nuclear reactors to streamline deployment. Introduced in New Jersey and referred to committee as of March 10, 2026.

bills.title: AI Definition statute
bills.ai_summary: Establishes a statutory definition of artificial intelligence for regulatory and legal purposes in Mississippi. Signed into law by Mississippi Governor on March 9, 2026.

bills.title: Data Center Non Disclosure Amendments
bills.ai_summary: Proposed amendments to restrict public disclosure of data center operating information to protect corporate confidentiality. Failed in Utah as of March 6, 2026.

bills.title: Protects residents from increased utility costs and utility shortages caused by data centers; clarifies authority to regulate
bills.ai_summary: Protects South Dakota residents from utility cost increases and shortages caused by data centers and clarifies state authority to regulate them. Passed South Dakota House 60-7 and enrolled as of March 5, 2026.

bills.title: AI in Election Campaign Material - Synthetic media disclosures
bills.ai_summary: Requires synthetic media (deepfakes) used in election campaigns to be clearly labeled and discloses AI-generated content. Signed into law by Vermont Governor Scott on March 5, 2026.

bills.title: AI Training Data Transparency Act (Senate companion)
bills.ai_summary: Requires AI developers to disclose what training data was used to build their models and allow data subjects to request removal. In New York Senate third reading as of March 4, 2026.

bills.title: Synthetic Content Provenance Disclosure Act
bills.ai_summary: Mandates disclosure labels on synthetic and AI-generated content to inform consumers about non-human creation. In New York Senate third reading as of March 4, 2026.

bills.title: Chief AI Officer position for state government
bills.ai_summary: Creates a Chief AI Officer position within New York state government to oversee AI policy and implementation. In New York Senate third reading as of March 4, 2026.

bills.title: Generative AI Accuracy Warning Disclosure requirement
bills.ai_summary: Requires AI systems to disclose accuracy limitations and potential errors in their outputs to users. In New York Senate third reading as of March 4, 2026.

bills.title: AI Mental Health Services Regulation
bills.ai_summary: Establishes regulatory standards for AI-powered mental health services, requiring clinical oversight and licensing compliance. Recommended for passage in Maine as of March 4, 2026.

bills.title: Child Access to AI Chatbots regulation
bills.ai_summary: Regulates child access to AI chatbots by requiring age verification and safety protections for minors. Tabled in Maine as of March 4, 2026.

bills.title: Data Center Amendments
bills.last_action: Recommends Returned to Rules in House Public Utilities and Energy Committee
bills.ai_summary: Proposes amendments to Utah data center regulations covering operational standards and community impact mitigation. Recommends return to House rules committee as of March 2, 2026.

bills.title: A bill to direct the FTC to establish requirements for making information available to the public about AI training data and algorithms
bills.ai_summary: Requires the FTC to establish public disclosure requirements for AI training data and algorithms used by companies. Currently referred to House Committee with no further action, meaning it has not advanced beyond initial assignment.

bills.title: Public hearings and disclosures prior to approval of data center development required
bills.ai_summary: Mandates public hearings and disclosure requirements before approving data center development projects in Minnesota. Still in committee referral stage with no advancement, so these requirements are not yet law.

bills.title: Environmental impact statement for data centers requirement provision
bills.ai_summary: Requires environmental impact statements to be completed before data center construction in Minnesota. Referred to committee and not yet advanced, meaning current data center projects may not require this assessment.

bills.title: Kids Chatbot Safety Act
bills.ai_summary: Aims to establish safety standards for chatbots marketed to children, likely restricting harmful content and protecting minors' privacy. Currently in Senate Finance Committee and has not passed, so no protections are yet in effect.

bills.title: California AI Standards and Safety Commission
bills.ai_summary: Creates a new California AI Standards and Safety Commission to develop and enforce AI safety regulations statewide. In Appropriations Committee, so funding and implementation details are still being determined.

bills.title: Chatbot Sexually Explicit Content Ban for minors
bills.ai_summary: Prohibits chatbots from displaying sexually explicit content to minors in California. Has passed one chamber and moved to the other, indicating significant legislative progress toward becoming law.

bills.title: Automated Decision Systems Inventory for state government
bills.ai_summary: Requires California state government to maintain an inventory of all automated decision systems it uses, improving transparency and accountability. Passed the initial chamber and advanced to Assembly, moving toward potential enactment.

bills.title: Critical Infrastructure Automated Decision Systems with human oversight
bills.ai_summary: Mandates human oversight for automated decision systems used in critical infrastructure to prevent fully autonomous harmful decisions. Currently in committee with no advancement, so these oversight requirements do not yet apply.

bills.title: Deepfake Prohibition in Advertising
bills.ai_summary: Prohibits the use of deepfake technology in advertisements in Hawaii to prevent consumer deception. Sent to Senate for consideration, representing moderate progress toward potential passage.

bills.title: Health Insurance AI Restrictions
bills.ai_summary: Restricts how health insurance companies can use AI in decision-making, likely requiring human review of coverage denials and rate-setting. Still in House committees without advancement, so no restrictions on insurance AI use are currently in effect.

bills.title: AI Surveillance Pricing Protection
bills.ai_summary: Protects Michigan consumers from AI-based price discrimination and surveillance by limiting how retailers can use AI to set individual prices. In House Economic Competitiveness Committee and has not advanced beyond referral.

bills.title: AI Safety Test Minimum Requirements
bills.ai_summary: Establishes minimum safety testing and validation requirements that AI systems must meet before deployment in New Jersey. In Senate Commerce Committee without advancement, so no mandatory AI safety testing is currently required.

bills.title: Generative AI Whistleblower Protections resolution
bills.ai_summary: Proposes protections for whistleblowers who report safety and ethical concerns about generative AI systems in New Jersey. A resolution in Senate Labor Committee, which typically carries less binding force than legislation.

bills.title: AI Medical Decisions Transparency Act
bills.ai_summary: Requires transparency and disclosure when AI is used to make medical decisions in Kansas, likely mandating doctors inform patients of AI involvement. Referred to Financial Institutions Committee, an unusual routing suggesting early-stage consideration.

bills.title: Educational Technology AI Privacy and Registration requirements
bills.ai_summary: Requires educational technology companies using AI to register with Vermont and protect student privacy. Currently in committee without advancement, so no registration or privacy requirements are yet enforced.

bills.title: Chatbot Data Protection and AI Disclosure requirements
bills.ai_summary: Mandates data protection standards for chatbots and requires companies to disclose when AI is being used in North Carolina. In committee with no advancement, so these disclosure and protection requirements are not yet law.

bills.title: AI and Synthetic Media in Elections - Materially deceptive media protections
bills.ai_summary: Protects election integrity by regulating deceptive synthetic media and deepfakes, requiring disclosure or prohibition of materially false election-related AI content. In committee stage with no advancement toward becoming law.

bills.title: Artificial Intelligence Electives in public schools
bills.ai_summary: Encourages schools to offer AI as an elective course in South Carolina to build student technical literacy. In committee, representing a non-regulatory education initiative rather than compliance requirements.

bills.title: Municipal data center zoning - noise limits, setbacks, electrical capacity requirements
bills.ai_summary: Establishes local zoning requirements for data centers including noise limits, setbacks from residential areas, and electrical capacity standards in New Hampshire municipalities. Engrossed status indicates it has passed initial chambers and is advanced in the legislative process.

bills.title: Establishes a new data centers moratorium and requires the Public Utility Commission to submit a report
bills.ai_summary: Imposes a moratorium on new data center construction in Minnesota pending a Public Utility Commission report on environmental and grid impacts. Referred to committee, so the moratorium is not yet in effect.

bills.title: Data Center Water Transparency Amendments
bills.ai_summary: Requires data centers in Utah to disclose their water usage and likely implement conservation measures or cleaner water sourcing practices. Engrossed status indicates strong legislative progress toward enactment.

bills.title: Certificates of Public Convenience and Necessity and Construction of Data Centers - Prohibits data center construction in certain Baltimore City development districts
bills.ai_summary: Blocks construction of new data centers in certain Baltimore City development districts unless they obtain a Certificate of Public Convenience and Necessity. Pending in Senate Rules Committee, so restrictions are not yet active but approaching potential passage.

bills.title: Retail Sales and Use Tax; exemption for data centers, delayed effective date - clean energy requirements
bills.ai_summary: Delays the effective date of a data center tax exemption in Virginia while adding clean energy requirements, meaning data centers face more regulatory conditions to qualify for tax relief. Already passed the House with a substantial majority, making it likely to become law.

bills.title: Water and energy use for data centers, including reporting and tariff requirements
bills.ai_summary: Imposes reporting and tariff requirements on Iowa data centers regarding water and energy consumption to manage resource impacts. Referred to committee without advancement, so no mandatory reporting is yet required.

bills.title: Imposes certain minimum requirements on data center customers in the state
bills.ai_summary: Establishes minimum operational standards for data center customers in Ohio, likely addressing energy efficiency, cooling systems, and resource usage. Newly introduced and referred to committee, representing early-stage consideration with no requirements yet in effect.

bills.title: Data centers; policy of the Commonwealth - encourages responsible operation supporting grid reliability and renewables
bills.ai_summary: Establishes Virginia policy encouraging responsible data center operations that support grid reliability and renewable energy integration. The bill has passed both chambers and is ready for executive action.

bills.title: Data Centers - Standards for Operation and Siting - Sound, vibration, backup power, and agricultural land restrictions
bills.last_action: Hearing scheduled for March 10 before House Environment and Transportation Committee
bills.ai_summary: Imposes operational standards on Maryland data centers including noise and vibration limits, backup power requirements, and restrictions on siting in agricultural areas. Currently scheduled for committee hearing, indicating early legislative stage.

bills.title: AI and Children's Internet Safety Study Commission
bills.ai_summary: Establishes an Alabama study commission to examine AI's impacts on children's internet safety and develop policy recommendations. The commission has been approved and will now conduct its research.

bills.title: Data center exemptions modified and certain defined terms maintained
bills.ai_summary: Modifies Minnesota's existing data center tax exemptions and clarifies certain regulatory definitions. The bill is in early committee review stage.

bills.title: Data Center Siting Act
bills.ai_summary: Creates a South Carolina framework for regulating data center siting and development standards. Currently referred to judiciary committee for evaluation.

bills.title: Imposes moratorium on issuance of permits for new data centers; requires PSC to minimize impact on electricity and gas rates
bills.ai_summary: Imposes a New York state moratorium on new data center permits and requires the Public Service Commission to evaluate and minimize impacts on electricity and gas utility rates. Still in committee with no floor votes yet.

bills.title: Imposes statewide moratorium on permits for new data centers; directs PSC to minimize impacts on electricity and gas rates
bills.ai_summary: Establishes a New York statewide moratorium on new data center permits and directs the PSC to minimize electricity and gas rate impacts from data center operations. Senate companion bill currently in committee referral.

bills.title: Data centers and certain data from water users; water use consumption
bills.ai_summary: Requires Virginia data centers to track and report water consumption to state water management authorities. The bill has passed both chambers and is ready for executive action.

bills.title: AI-Generated Child Exploitation Images criminalization
bills.ai_summary: Criminalizes the creation, possession, and distribution of AI-generated child sexual abuse material in Kansas. This bill has been signed into law and is now effective.

bills.title: Repeals provisions relating to Computer Data Center Equipment Incentive Program
bills.ai_summary: Eliminates Pennsylvania's Computer Data Center Equipment Incentive Program, removing tax incentives for data center investment. Currently in committee for consideration.

bills.title: ICE Out of Our Faces Act - Ban ICE and CBP use of facial recognition
bills.ai_summary: Bans Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Customs and Border Protection from using facial recognition technology in federal operations. Introduced in Senate and awaiting committee assignment.

bills.title: Resolution to urge temporary pause on discretionary state-level incentives for data center construction and comprehensive review of impacts
bills.last_action: Pending in House Government Operations Committee
bills.ai_summary: Michigan resolution urging a temporary pause on state discretionary incentives for data center construction and a comprehensive review of economic and environmental impacts. Pending in House committee with no floor action.

bills.title: Office of Transformation and Opportunity; AI, Data Center and Emerging Technology Regulatory Sandbox Program; permits for high impact data centers
bills.last_action: Amended in Senate Committee on Communications & Technology
bills.ai_summary: Creates a Pennsylvania regulatory sandbox program for AI and data center technologies, establishing expedited permitting for high-impact data center projects. Recently amended in Senate committee and advancing in the legislative process.

bills.title: Imposes one-year moratorium on the construction or expansion of hyperscale data centers
bills.ai_summary: Imposes a one-year moratorium on construction and expansion of hyperscale data centers in South Dakota. Currently in committee referral stage.

bills.title: Data Center Impact Assessment and Development Moratorium Act of 2026 - Prohibits the construction or development of new data centers for a specified time
bills.last_action: Referred to House Energy, Utilities & Telecommunications Committee
bills.ai_summary: Prohibits construction and development of new data centers in Georgia for a specified timeframe and requires impact assessments before approval. Referred to energy and utilities committee.

bills.title: Requires electric utilities to protect residential and retail electricity customers from costs associated with data center construction and operation
bills.ai_summary: Requires Georgia electric utilities to shield residential and retail customers from cost increases caused by data center construction and operations. In committee review stage.

bills.title: Data centers - defining terms and requiring Corporation Commission to provide classification of service
bills.ai_summary: Requires Oklahoma's Corporation Commission to classify and define data center service categories and establish regulatory standards. Recently introduced and under committee consideration.

bills.title: Allows Public Utilities Commission to assess actual costs to data centers
bills.ai_summary: Allows South Dakota's Public Utilities Commission to assess actual operational costs against data centers rather than using flat rates. Passed both chambers and is ready for executive action.

bills.title: Prohibits nondisclosure of certain agreements related to data centers
bills.ai_summary: Would require disclosure of confidential agreements related to data centers in South Dakota. Failed to pass the House by a narrow margin (30-37 vote).

bills.title: Data centers: monthly reporting
bills.ai_summary: Requires California data centers to submit monthly operational reports to state regulators for transparency and monitoring purposes. Introduced and referred to committee.

bills.title: Large-Load Data Centers
bills.last_action: Senate Committee on Transportation & Energy - amendments failed
bills.ai_summary: Regulates large-load data centers in Colorado with environmental and operational standards. Failed proposed amendments in Senate committee, stalling further progress.

bills.title: Comprehensive environmental, water, and energy regulations for hyperscale data centers including cumulative impact assessments and Water Impact Permits
bills.ai_summary: Establishes comprehensive environmental, water, and energy regulations for Illinois hyperscale data centers including cumulative impact assessments and water permitting requirements. Referred to committee for detailed review.

bills.title: Data center sales and use tax exemptions contingent upon contributions to state higher education institutions
bills.ai_summary: Conditions Iowa's data center sales and use tax exemptions on facility contributions to state higher education institutions. Referred to committee for evaluation.

bills.title: Data Center Planning and Transparency Act
bills.ai_summary: Establishes Maryland data center planning and transparency requirements for utilities and local governments. In early committee referral stage.

bills.title: Data centers - energy administration, utility regulation, fees, and permits
bills.ai_summary: Regulates Maryland data center energy administration, utility regulation, permitting, and fees to manage costs and environmental impacts. Referred to committee for consideration.

bills.title: Data centers provisions modification
bills.ai_summary: Modifies Minnesota's data center regulatory provisions, though specific policy changes are not detailed in available information. The bill is in early committee review stage.

bills.title: Limits costs and risks associated with electricity use by data centers and imposes a moratorium
bills.ai_summary: Imposes a moratorium on new data center construction in South Dakota while imposing cost and risk limitations on electricity use by existing data centers. Currently in committee review.

bills.title: Transparency in Frontier AI Act
bills.ai_summary: Requires transparency and disclosure requirements for companies developing frontier AI models in Illinois. Bill was just introduced and has not yet advanced.

bills.title: AI Provenance Data Act
bills.ai_summary: Establishes data provenance tracking requirements for AI systems used in Illinois, requiring documentation of training data sources and lineage. Bill is in early introduction stage.

bills.title: AI Safety Act
bills.ai_summary: Creates comprehensive safety and testing standards for AI systems deployed in Illinois, including pre-deployment auditing requirements. Bill was recently introduced with no committee action yet.

bills.title: Future of Artificial Intelligence Innovation Act of 2026
bills.ai_summary: Federal legislation promoting AI innovation while establishing baseline safety and accountability frameworks for frontier AI model development. Currently in Senate committee review.

bills.title: AI Non-Sentience and Responsibility Act
bills.ai_summary: Establishes legal responsibility frameworks for AI systems in Missouri, clarifying that AI cannot achieve sentience and defining liability for AI-caused harms. Bill is under review in the General Laws Committee.

bills.title: AI Risk Management Transparency Act
bills.ai_summary: Requires Nebraska AI developers and deployers to publicly disclose risk management practices and safety measures for their systems. Bill is in the Banking/Commerce/Insurance Committee.

bills.title: AI Data Privacy Act
bills.ai_summary: Establishes data privacy protections for individuals whose personal information is used to train or operate AI systems in Illinois. Bill was just introduced without committee assignment.

bills.title: AI Safety Measures Act
bills.ai_summary: Mandates specific AI safety measures including testing, bias audits, and human oversight requirements for AI systems used in Illinois. Bill is in early introduction stage.

bills.title: AI Design Requirements Act
bills.ai_summary: Sets design and architecture requirements for AI systems deployed in Illinois, including explainability and auditability standards. Bill was recently introduced with no further action.

bills.title: Nursing AI Use and Practice Act amendment
bills.ai_summary: Amends Illinois nursing practice laws to clarify scope of AI use in nursing care and establish guidelines for AI-assisted medical decisions. Bill is in early introduction stage.

bills.title: Property Owner Protection from Data Center Impacts Act - Compensation for harm caused by construction or operation of data centers within 1,000 feet
bills.ai_summary: Requires data center operators to compensate property owners for documented harm (property damage, environmental impact, etc.) caused by data center construction or operation within 1,000 feet. Bill has advanced to the House Rules Committee.

bills.title: Prohibits local governments from issuing permits, licenses, or certificates for new data center construction until March 1, 2027
bills.ai_summary: Implements a statewide moratorium in Georgia prohibiting local governments from issuing permits for new data center construction until March 1, 2027. Bill is in early committee review.

bills.title: Removing tax exemption for the replacement of equipment for data centers
bills.ai_summary: Removes existing tax exemptions in Washington for data center equipment replacement, increasing tax liability for data center operators. Bill has a public hearing scheduled, advancing toward floor consideration.

bills.title: Water resources: data centers - Requires data center operators to report expected water use to water suppliers before applying for business license
bills.ai_summary: Would have required California data center operators to report water use projections to local water suppliers before obtaining business licenses, but was vetoed by the Governor. Bill is dead for this session.

bills.title: Moratorium on data centers
bills.ai_summary: Imposes a moratorium on new data center construction in Wisconsin, halting expansion of the data center industry. Bill is in early committee review.

bills.title: Moratorium on data centers (Assembly companion)
bills.ai_summary: Assembly companion bill to Wisconsin's data center moratorium (SB1061), imposing the same halt on new data center construction. Bill is in early committee review.

bills.title: Approval of Data Center Facilities
bills.ai_summary: Establishes approval process and requirements for new data center facility construction in Florida, likely including environmental and infrastructure standards. Bill is being reviewed in the Economic Infrastructure Subcommittee.

bills.title: Directs SCC to evaluate impact of requiring data centers to limit Tier 2 generators and prioritize Tier 4 equivalent
bills.ai_summary: Directs Virginia's State Corporation Commission to study the feasibility and impact of requiring data centers to use cleaner Tier 4 equivalent generators instead of higher-emission Tier 2 units. Bill is in early committee review.

bills.title: Data centers energy generation and storage; report requirement
bills.ai_summary: Requires Virginia data centers to report on energy generation and storage practices and mandates a study of environmental and infrastructure impacts. Bill is in early committee review.

bills.title: Emerging large energy use facilities
bills.ai_summary: Establishes regulatory oversight for emerging large energy-use facilities (primarily data centers) in Washington, likely including permitting and environmental requirements. Bill is in early committee review.

bills.title: Retail sales and use tax exemption for construction and equipping of new data centers in eastern Washington counties
bills.ai_summary: Provides retail sales tax exemptions for construction and equipment of new data centers in eastern Washington counties, creating a financial incentive for data center development. Bill is in early committee review.

bills.title: Prohibits nondisclosure agreements intended to conceal information about a data center from the public
bills.ai_summary: Prohibits data center operators from using nondisclosure agreements to conceal information about their operations from the public, promoting transparency. Bill is in early committee review.

bills.title: Enhanced RAISE Act - Transparency and safety for AI frontier model developers
bills.ai_summary: Enhances New York's RAISE Act to require frontier AI model developers to disclose safety testing and risk mitigation measures to regulators and the public. Bill was recently introduced in the Senate.

bills.title: Age-Appropriate Design Code Act
bills.ai_summary: Requires online platforms and services to design features in an age-appropriate manner and implement safeguards for minors, addressing issues like addictive design and privacy protection. The bill has passed the South Carolina House and awaits the Governor's decision.

bills.title: Requires energy usage plans for proposed AI data centers and cryptocurrency mining facilities; requires clean energy sources
bills.ai_summary: Mandates that proposed AI data centers and cryptocurrency mining facilities in New Jersey submit detailed energy usage plans and demonstrate use of clean energy sources. Currently in early legislative stage, referred to committee for consideration.

bills.title: Requires electric public utilities to develop and submit tariff for data centers with monthly demand of at least 100 MW
bills.ai_summary: Requires New Jersey electric utilities to develop special tariff rates for data centers consuming at least 100 MW monthly, establishing regulatory framework for large-scale data center power agreements. Bill is in initial committee review phase.

bills.title: Tennessee Data Center Impact Review Act
bills.ai_summary: Establishes an impact review process for data center projects in Tennessee to assess infrastructure, environmental, and community effects before approval. Currently referred to committee with no further action taken.

bills.title: Requires data center owner/operator to pay full cost of infrastructure needed to support the data center
bills.ai_summary: Requires Tennessee data center owners and operators to pay the full cost of all infrastructure (roads, utilities, drainage) needed to support their facilities rather than passing costs to local governments or ratepayers. Bill is in committee referral stage.

bills.title: Requires certain data centers to register with department of revenue and utilities to report usage and rates
bills.ai_summary: Requires certain Tennessee data centers to register with the state and report their electricity usage and utility rates to increase transparency and regulatory oversight. Bill is in early committee stage.

bills.title: Data centers - requires owner/operator to pay full cost of infrastructure (crossfile of HB1847)
bills.ai_summary: A companion bill to HB1847 that requires Tennessee data center owners and operators to bear full infrastructure costs needed to support their operations. Currently referred to committee for review.

bills.title: Prohibits governmental entities from granting final approval for new data center applications, permits, or incentives until January 1, 2028
bills.ai_summary: Imposes a moratorium in South Carolina prohibiting government entities from approving new data center applications, permits, or providing incentives until January 1, 2028, effectively halting data center expansion for nearly two years. Bill is in committee review.

bills.title: Data Center Utility Costs
bills.ai_summary: Addresses utility cost allocation for commercial data centers in South Carolina, likely aiming to prevent data centers from shifting costs to residential and small business ratepayers. Currently in committee.

bills.title: Commercial Data Center Water Usage Report
bills.ai_summary: Requires commercial data centers in South Carolina to report their water usage, enabling the state to track and monitor the water consumption impact of data center operations. Bill referred to Judiciary Committee.

bills.title: Sales and use tax exemption for goods and services related to data center operations
bills.ai_summary: Provides sales and use tax exemptions for equipment and services directly related to data center operations in South Dakota, reducing operational costs for data center developers. Advanced past committee with bipartisan support (9-3 vote).

bills.title: Artificial Intelligence Data Center Environmental Accountability Act
bills.ai_summary: Establishes environmental accountability standards for AI data centers in Missouri, requiring emissions reductions or environmental mitigation measures. Currently in early House floor stage.

bills.title: Requires hyperscale data centers to offset electricity demand by funding household electrification - heat pumps, solar, battery storage
bills.ai_summary: Requires hyperscale data centers in New York to offset their electricity demand by funding household electrification projects including heat pumps, solar panels, and battery storage systems. Bill referred to committee for consideration.

bills.title: Temporary moratorium on AI data centers and a report on construction and operation of AI data centers
bills.last_action: Read 1st time and referred to Committee on Finance
bills.ai_summary: Imposes a temporary moratorium on new AI data center construction and operation in Vermont while the state studies their construction impacts and operational effects. Currently in Finance Committee following first reading.

bills.title: Annual reporting of energy consumption and water consumption by data centers
bills.ai_summary: Requires Pennsylvania data centers to submit annual reports on energy and water consumption, creating transparency for environmental and resource impact tracking. Bill referred to committee in early January.

bills.title: Data center ordinance assistance
bills.ai_summary: Provides technical and financial assistance to Pennsylvania municipalities and counties in developing or implementing data center ordinances. Referred to committee for review.

bills.title: Responsible AI Safety and Education (RAISE) Act - Frontier model transparency and safety
bills.ai_summary: New York's RAISE Act, now law, requires developers of frontier AI models to provide transparency on model capabilities, training data, and safety testing results to state regulators. Signed into law by Governor Hochul in December 2025.

bills.title: READ AI Models Act - AI model documentation best practices
bills.ai_summary: Establishes best practices and documentation standards for AI model development and deployment at the federal level to improve transparency and safety. Referred to House committee for consideration.

bills.title: Certain requirements related to data centers
bills.ai_summary: Establishes requirements for data center operations in Wisconsin, likely addressing infrastructure, environmental, or utility issues related to large-scale data centers. Currently in committee referral stage.

bills.title: AI for America Act - National strategy for AI leadership and regulatory barrier removal
bills.ai_summary: Proposes a national AI strategy and seeks to remove regulatory barriers to AI development and deployment, positioning the U.S. for AI leadership. Referred to House committee.

bills.title: Federal Agency Technology and AI Talent Teams Act
bills.ai_summary: Establishes federal inter-agency task forces to recruit and retain AI and technology talent within government agencies. Referred to Senate committee.

bills.title: Grid modernization surcharge on data centers meeting specified electricity threshold; establishes grid modernization fund
bills.ai_summary: Creates a grid modernization surcharge on New York data centers that exceed specified electricity thresholds, with revenues directed to a grid modernization fund. Referred to committee in October.

bills.title: Accountability of Costs for Data Centers Act - Independent classification of service for large energy use facilities
bills.ai_summary: Requires independent classification of large energy-consuming facilities like data centers to ensure cost accountability and prevent cost-shifting to residential ratepayers. Referred to Senate committee in October.

bills.title: Ratepayer and Technological Innovation Protection Act - Establishes special rate structure for data centers to protect residential ratepayers and small businesses
bills.last_action: Chaptered by Secretary of State as Chapter 647, Statutes of 2025
bills.ai_summary: Now California law, establishes special rate structures for data centers to protect residential customers and small businesses from bearing disproportionate utility costs. Approved and chaptered as law in October 2025.

bills.title: Amends Tax Reform Code - Computer Data Center Equipment Incentive Program - application and eligibility requirements
bills.ai_summary: Amends Pennsylvania's Tax Reform Code to modify the Computer Data Center Equipment Incentive Program, adjusting application requirements and eligibility criteria for tax incentives. Referred to committee for review in October 2025.

bills.title: Large energy customer fees, renewable resource tariff, building requirements for data centers, water usage, required pay rate on data center projects
bills.ai_summary: Wisconsin bill that establishes fees for large energy customers, sets renewable resource tariffs, and imposes building requirements, water usage standards, and prevailing wage mandates for data center projects. Currently in committee and has not advanced.

bills.title: Large energy customer fees; renewable resource tariff; building requirements for data centers; water usage; pay rate (Senate companion of AB722)
bills.ai_summary: Senate companion to WI AB722 with identical provisions regulating large energy customer fees, renewable tariffs, data center building standards, water usage, and prevailing wage requirements. Currently in committee with no advancement.

bills.title: GAIN AI Act of 2025 (Senate companion)
bills.ai_summary: Federal Senate bill aimed at guaranteeing access to and innovation in artificial intelligence development and deployment. Currently in committee with unclear specific policy mechanisms from title alone.

bills.title: Advanced Artificial Intelligence Security Readiness Act of 2025
bills.ai_summary: Federal bill establishing security readiness standards and evaluation requirements for advanced artificial intelligence systems. Currently in committee pending further action.

bills.title: Regulation of commercial data centers; duties on PUC, DHS, and PA Energy Development Authority; Data Center Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program
bills.ai_summary: Pennsylvania bill regulating commercial data centers through PUC and state agency oversight and establishes a Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program for areas affected by data center operations. Currently in committee as of September 2025.

bills.title: Personalized Algorithmic Pricing Disclosure Act
bills.ai_summary: New York law now in effect requiring retailers and e-commerce platforms to disclose when they use algorithmic pricing and allow consumers to opt out or access non-personalized pricing. This directly impacts consumer protections against dynamic pricing discrimination.

bills.title: Transparency in Frontier Artificial Intelligence Act (TFAIA)
bills.ai_summary: California law signed by Governor requiring transparency reports from frontier AI developers on model capabilities, limitations, and safety testing before deployment. This establishes baseline disclosure requirements for advanced AI systems.

bills.title: Generative AI Training Data Transparency Act - Requires disclosure of training data
bills.ai_summary: California law effective January 1, 2026 requiring AI developers to disclose the sources and characteristics of data used to train generative AI models. This addresses concerns about data provenance and intellectual property in AI training.

bills.title: AI Content Transparency - Disclosure requirements for AI-generated content
bills.ai_summary: California law requiring clear disclosure when content is AI-generated across platforms and media to combat misinformation and deception. This transparency requirement is now in effect.

bills.title: Companion Chatbot Regulation and Disclosure Requirements
bills.ai_summary: California law requiring AI chatbots and companion systems to disclose they are AI, clearly explain their limitations, and not form inappropriate relationships with users. Now in effect to protect vulnerable users.

bills.title: Sexual Deepfakes Criminalization
bills.ai_summary: California law criminalizing the creation and distribution of non-consensual sexual deepfakes without consent to address intimate image abuse. Now enforceable as a criminal offense.

bills.title: Deepfake Nudes Reporting Requirements for platforms
bills.ai_summary: California law requiring online platforms to implement reporting mechanisms and processes for deepfake nude images and to remove them expeditiously. Now in effect to combat non-consensual intimate imagery.

bills.title: AI-Generated Child Sexual Abuse Material (CSAM) criminalization
bills.ai_summary: California law criminalizing the creation and distribution of AI-generated child sexual abuse material (CSAM), treating it as a felony offense. Now enforceable to prevent AI-facilitated child exploitation.

bills.title: Digital Replicas of Deceased Performers Protection
bills.ai_summary: California law protecting the digital likenesses and personas of deceased performers from unauthorized commercial use through estate rights. Now in effect to protect performer legacies and family interests.

bills.title: Defending Democracy from Deepfake Deception Act - Election deepfake prohibitions
bills.ai_summary: California law prohibiting the creation and distribution of election deepfakes that mislead voters about candidates or voting procedures close to elections. Now enforceable to protect election integrity.

bills.title: Political Advertisement AI Disclosures
bills.ai_summary: California law requiring political advertisements using AI-generated content to disclose the use of AI and provide methods for viewers to identify AI-generated material. Now in effect for 2026 elections and beyond.

bills.title: Automated Decision Systems Accountability Act - Regulates consequential ADS decisions
bills.ai_summary: California bill regulating automated decision systems that make consequential decisions about employment, housing, credit, and benefits, requiring impact assessments and bias testing. Designated as two-year bill, returning for consideration in 2026.

bills.title: GAIN AI Act of 2025 - Guaranteeing Access and Innovation for National AI
bills.ai_summary: Federal House bill aimed at guaranteeing access to and innovation in artificial intelligence development across the United States. Currently in committee pending debate and potential floor action.

bills.title: Artificial Intelligence Risk Evaluation Act of 2025
bills.ai_summary: Federal bill requiring evaluation and risk assessment frameworks for artificial intelligence systems before deployment in high-impact applications. Currently in committee with no committee action to date.

bills.title: Synthetic Media Likeness Protection - Civil remedies and registration requirements
bills.ai_summary: New York law now in effect allowing individuals to register their likeness and pursue civil remedies against unauthorized creation and use of synthetic media using their image. This creates private rights of action for deepfake harm.

bills.title: Wellness and Oversight for Psychological Resources Act - AI therapy restrictions
bills.ai_summary: Illinois law signed by Governor prohibiting the use of AI-based therapy or mental health treatment for certain vulnerable populations and establishing oversight requirements for AI mental health tools. Now in effect to protect patient safety.

bills.title: Data center regulatory bill (1st Special Session)
bills.ai_summary: Minnesota law passed during special session establishing comprehensive regulatory framework for data centers including operational standards, environmental requirements, and local authority provisions. Now in effect as Chapter 12.

bills.title: Colorado AI Act Implementation Delay - Postpones SB 24-205 to June 30, 2026
bills.ai_summary: Colorado law signed by Governor Polis delaying implementation of the state's comprehensive AI Act (SB 24-205) from January 1, 2026 to June 30, 2026 to allow more time for rule development. Implementation now postponed approximately six months.

bills.title: Artificial Intelligence Policy Act Extension to July 1, 2027
bills.ai_summary: Utah law extending the sunset date of the state's Artificial Intelligence Policy Act from July 1, 2025 to July 1, 2027 to continue regulatory oversight and data collection on AI impacts. This ensures continued state-level AI governance.

bills.title: American Artificial Intelligence Leadership and Uniformity Act
bills.ai_summary: Federal bill proposing uniform national standards for AI development, deployment, and regulation to prevent state-by-state fragmentation and promote American competitiveness. Currently in committee without further action.

bills.title: PROACTIV Artificial Intelligence Data Act of 2025
bills.ai_summary: Establishes requirements for proactive oversight and governance of artificial intelligence data practices at the federal level. Currently in committee with no enacted status, representing an early-stage proposal for federal AI regulation.

bills.title: Facial Recognition Act of 2025
bills.ai_summary: Proposes federal restrictions on facial recognition technology use and deployment. Remains in committee as of July 2025 with no enacted effect on policy.

bills.title: Prohibition on AI Professors in Community College Programming
bills.ai_summary: Prohibits community colleges in Illinois from employing AI systems or algorithms to teach programming courses, requiring human instructors instead. Enrolled and sent to Governor on June 24, 2025, indicating imminent implementation pending gubernatorial signature.

bills.title: Texas Responsible Artificial Intelligence Governance Act (TRAIGA)
bills.ai_summary: Creates comprehensive AI governance framework for Texas state government including standards, oversight mechanisms, and accountability measures. Signed into law by Governor Abbott on June 22, 2025, and now in effect.

bills.title: Nonconsensual Intimate Deepfake Materials - Criminalization and civil cause of action
bills.ai_summary: Criminalizes creation and distribution of non-consensual intimate deepfakes and creates civil liability for victims in Texas. Signed into law on June 20, 2025, providing new criminal penalties and civil remedies.

bills.title: Social Media Deepfake Removal - Requires platforms to provide complaint systems and timely removal
bills.ai_summary: Mandates social media platforms maintain complaint systems for deepfake content and remove verified non-consensual intimate deepfakes within a specified timeframe. Signed into law on June 20, 2025, creating platform compliance obligations.

bills.title: Catfishing and AI Impersonation - Civil liability for impersonating others on social media
bills.ai_summary: Establishes civil liability in Texas for using AI or deception to impersonate others on social media platforms without consent. Signed into law on June 20, 2025, enabling victims to pursue damages.

bills.title: AI Sexual Material Safeguards - Age verification on sites offering AI content involving minors
bills.ai_summary: Requires age verification systems on websites offering AI-generated sexual content involving minors and prohibits such content in Texas. Signed into law on June 20, 2025, implementing protective safeguards against AI-generated child sexual abuse material.

bills.title: Electronic Health Records and AI in Diagnostics - Requires US-based storage and AI disclosure
bills.ai_summary: Mandates electronic health records be stored on U.S.-based servers and requires healthcare providers to disclose use of AI in diagnostics to patients in Texas. Signed into law on June 20, 2025, establishing data residency and AI transparency requirements.

bills.title: Government AI Use Standards - AI inventory, ethics code, and risk assessments for state agencies
bills.ai_summary: Requires Texas state agencies to maintain AI inventories, adopt ethics codes, and conduct risk assessments for all AI systems they deploy. Signed into law on June 20, 2025, establishing accountability and oversight mechanisms for government AI use.

bills.title: AI Division Creation within Department of Information Resources
bills.ai_summary: Creates a dedicated AI Division within Texas's Department of Information Resources to centralize state AI policy and governance. Signed into law on June 20, 2025, establishing institutional capacity for AI oversight.

bills.title: AI and Cybersecurity Training for state and local government employees
bills.ai_summary: Mandates AI and cybersecurity training programs for state and local government employees in Texas. Signed into law on June 20, 2025, requiring workforce development in AI competency.

bills.title: An Act Requiring A Study Concerning Energy Efficiency Requirements For Artificial Intelligence Data Centers
bills.ai_summary: Proposed a study on energy efficiency requirements for AI data centers in Connecticut to address computational resource consumption. Died in committee on June 4, 2025, and will not advance without reintroduction.

bills.title: New York Artificial Intelligence Act - Regulates AI systems to prevent algorithmic discrimination
bills.ai_summary: Proposes comprehensive New York regulation of AI systems to prevent algorithmic discrimination and bias in decision-making. Currently in committee as of June 1, 2025, with no enacted effect.

bills.title: New York Artificial Intelligence Act - Regulates development and use of AI systems
bills.ai_summary: Proposes New York legislation regulating the development, deployment, and use of AI systems across sectors. Currently in committee as of June 1, 2025, with no enacted effect.

bills.title: Ban on biometric identifying technology including facial recognition in schools
bills.ai_summary: Proposes banning biometric identifying technology including facial recognition in New York schools to protect student privacy. Currently in committee as of June 1, 2025, with no enacted effect.

bills.title: High-Risk AI Systems - Information security and deployment requirements
bills.ai_summary: Proposes California requirements for information security and deployment standards for high-risk AI systems. Currently in committee as of June 1, 2025, with no enacted effect.

bills.title: No Robo Bosses Act - AI in employment automated decisions
bills.ai_summary: Proposes California restrictions on automated AI decision-making in employment contexts to protect workers. Currently in committee as of June 1, 2025, with no enacted effect.

bills.title: Law Enforcement Agencies and Artificial Intelligence regulation
bills.ai_summary: Proposes California regulation of law enforcement use of AI systems including restrictions and oversight mechanisms. Currently in committee as of June 1, 2025, with no enacted effect.

bills.title: Health Care Services and AI - Notification requirements
bills.ai_summary: Proposes California requirements for healthcare providers to notify patients when AI is used in treatment decisions and services. Currently in committee as of June 1, 2025, with no enacted effect.

bills.title: Automated Decision Systems impact assessments for private sector
bills.ai_summary: Proposes California requirement for private sector organizations to conduct impact assessments of automated decision systems. Currently in committee as of June 1, 2025, with no enacted effect.

bills.title: Indistinguishable Visual Depiction - AI-generated child exploitation imagery
bills.ai_summary: Criminalizes creation and distribution of AI-generated child sexual abuse material in Arizona. Enacted on June 1, 2025, establishing criminal penalties for this offense.

bills.title: Penalties for fraudulent AI-generated voice recordings
bills.ai_summary: Criminalizes creation of fraudulent AI-generated voice recordings impersonating others in Arizona. Enacted on June 1, 2025, creating new criminal liability for voice-based AI fraud.

bills.title: Criminal liability for AI-generated intimate content
bills.ai_summary: Establishes criminal liability in Arizona for creating and distributing AI-generated intimate content without consent. Enacted on June 1, 2025, criminalizing non-consensual AI-generated sexual imagery.

bills.title: Claims and Prior Authorization using AI in healthcare
bills.ai_summary: Regulates use of AI in healthcare claims processing and prior authorization decisions in Arizona to ensure transparency and fairness. Enacted on June 1, 2025, governing AI deployment in insurance and healthcare administration.

bills.title: AI Studies and Education Use in Labor and Employment
bills.ai_summary: AL H365 requires AI studies and education programs focused on labor and employment impacts, now law as of June 2025. This establishes educational initiatives to prepare workers for AI-driven workplace changes.

bills.title: AI Commission Establishment
bills.ai_summary: DE HB16 creates a state AI Commission to oversee AI policy and regulation in Delaware, effective immediately. The commission will develop frameworks for responsible AI deployment across sectors.

bills.title: Pediatric Rare Diseases - AI in healthcare diagnostics
bills.ai_summary: FL H907 authorizes AI use in pediatric rare disease diagnosis within healthcare settings, now enacted. This enables hospitals and clinicians to deploy diagnostic AI tools for rare pediatric conditions.

bills.title: Automation Workforce Impact Study
bills.ai_summary: FL H827 would have mandated a study on automation's workforce impact but was vetoed by the Governor on June 1, 2025. The state will not conduct the requested labor market analysis.

bills.title: Health Insurance Patient AI protections
bills.ai_summary: CT SB10 establishes protections for health insurance patients against unfair AI-based decision-making in coverage determinations, now law. Insurers must disclose when AI influences claim denials and provide appeal rights.

bills.title: Pricing Coordination through AI Algorithms prohibition
bills.ai_summary: CO HB1004 would have prohibited AI algorithms from coordinating prices between competitors but was vetoed by Governor Polis on June 1, 2025. Price-fixing through AI remains unregulated under this measure.

bills.title: Intimate Digital Depictions criminalization
bills.ai_summary: CO SB288 criminalizes creation and distribution of non-consensual intimate digital images and deepfake pornography, now law. Violators face criminal penalties for generating or sharing sexually explicit synthetic media without consent.

bills.title: Nonconsensual Intimate Deepfakes prohibition
bills.ai_summary: MI HB4047 criminalizes creation and distribution of non-consensual intimate deepfakes, effective immediately. Violations are felonies with prison time and fines up to $100,000.

bills.title: Nonconsensual Intimate Deepfakes (companion bill)
bills.ai_summary: MI HB4048 is a companion bill to HB4047 providing additional protections against non-consensual intimate deepfakes, now enacted. It strengthens enforcement mechanisms and victim remedies.

bills.title: Nonconsensual Intimate Images using AI
bills.ai_summary: MN HF971 criminalizes creation and distribution of non-consensual intimate images created or manipulated with AI, now law. Victims can pursue civil remedies and perpetrators face criminal charges.

bills.title: AI Workforce Development Program
bills.ai_summary: MN SF1845 establishes a workforce development program to train workers in AI skills and prepare labor markets for automation, effective June 2025. Funding supports education and reskilling initiatives.

bills.title: Nonconsensual Intimate Images using AI
bills.ai_summary: MO HB1074 criminalizes creation and distribution of non-consensual intimate images using AI deepfake technology, now law. Penalties include fines and imprisonment for violations.

bills.title: Deepfake Distribution Prohibition
