bills.ai_summary: NE LB615 would prohibit distribution of deepfakes but remains in introduction stage as of June 2025. The bill has not advanced through the legislature.

bills.title: AI-Generated CSAM Prohibition
bills.ai_summary: NE LB172 would ban creation and distribution of AI-generated child sexual abuse material, currently introduced. No legislative action has occurred yet.

bills.title: Algorithm Discrimination in AI prohibition
bills.ai_summary: NE LB642 would prohibit discriminatory algorithmic decision-making in AI systems, still in introduction. The bill aims to prevent illegal discrimination in hiring, lending, and housing.

bills.title: Nonconsensual Intimate Images using AI
bills.ai_summary: ME LD125 criminalizes creation and distribution of non-consensual intimate images using AI, now enacted. Maine joins other states in protecting individuals from synthetic intimate abuse.

bills.title: Nonconsensual Deepfake Pornography criminalization
bills.ai_summary: MD HB154 criminalizes creation and distribution of non-consensual deepfake pornography, now law. Penalties apply to anyone making or sharing intimate synthetic media without consent.

bills.title: Private Sector AI Study Group
bills.ai_summary: MD HB956 establishes a study group to examine private sector AI practices and recommend regulations, effective immediately. The group will report findings to policymakers for future legislation.

bills.title: Deepfake Laws - Synthetic media involving minors and manipulated depictions
bills.ai_summary: KS SB186 criminalizes synthetic media depicting minors in sexual situations and manipulated depictions of real people, now law. Penalties target child exploitation and fraudulent deepfakes.

bills.title: Synthetic Media Involving Minors criminalization
bills.ai_summary: IN HB1047 criminalizes creation of synthetic media depicting minors in sexual or violent content, now enacted. Violators face felony charges with significant prison sentences.

bills.title: Political Synthetic Media disclosure requirements
bills.ai_summary: IN HB1133 requires disclosure labels on political synthetic media and deepfakes before election, now law. Politicians and campaigns must notify voters of AI-generated content.

bills.title: AI Use in Healthcare Disclosure - Patient and insurer notification requirements
bills.ai_summary: IN HB1620 requires healthcare providers and insurers to disclose when AI is used in patient care or coverage decisions, currently in committee. The bill has not been voted on yet.

bills.title: AI Commission establishment
bills.ai_summary: VT S197 establishes a state AI Commission to develop AI governance policy and coordinate regulation, effective immediately. The commission will advise the legislature on AI matters.

bills.title: AI Task Force for Public Sector
bills.ai_summary: WV HB3187 creates an AI Task Force to examine AI use in public sector agencies and recommend oversight policies, now law. The task force will report findings to improve government AI practices.

bills.title: AI Advisory Council establishment
bills.ai_summary: VA HB2360 establishes an AI Advisory Council to develop recommendations for AI governance and policy in Virginia, now enacted. The council will guide the state's AI strategy and regulation.

bills.title: Deepfake Disclosure in elections
bills.ai_summary: Requires disclosure when deepfake media is used in election communications in Alaska. Currently stalled in committee as of June 2025, meaning it has not advanced toward passage.

bills.title: Synthetic Media Liability
bills.ai_summary: Establishes liability standards for creators and distributors of synthetic media in Alaska. Currently stalled in committee as of June 2025 with no clear path to enactment.

bills.title: AI Legislative Task Force creation
bills.ai_summary: Creates a legislative task force in Alaska to study AI policy and make recommendations. Currently stalled in committee as of June 2025.

bills.title: Language Access Assessment using AI
bills.ai_summary: Authorizes Colorado to use AI tools to assess language access needs for state services and improve access for non-English speakers. Now law as of June 2025.

bills.title: E-Discovery Task Force - AI in litigation
bills.ai_summary: Establishes a task force in Colorado to study how AI should be used and regulated in e-discovery for litigation. Now law as of June 2025.

bills.title: Blockchain and AI Technology government integration
bills.ai_summary: Authorizes Arizona state agencies to integrate blockchain and AI technology into government operations. Now law as of June 2025.

bills.title: AI Advisory Council establishment
bills.ai_summary: Creates an AI Advisory Council in Texas to advise state leaders on AI policy and regulation. Now law as of June 2025.

bills.title: AI Impact Assessments for Public Services
bills.ai_summary: Requires Texas state agencies to conduct impact assessments before deploying AI in public services, ensuring accountability for algorithmic decisions. Now law as of June 2025.

bills.title: Licensed Professional Titles Protection - AI cannot use medical professional titles
bills.ai_summary: Prohibits AI systems from using protected medical professional titles in Oregon without a licensed practitioner. Now law as of June 2025, preventing AI from falsely claiming to be doctors or nurses.

bills.title: Insurance AI Regulation
bills.ai_summary: Establishes new regulatory requirements for insurance companies using AI in Connecticut, including transparency and fairness standards. Now law as of June 2025.

bills.title: California AI Transparency Act
bills.ai_summary: Would require AI developers and deployers in California to disclose how their systems work and their impacts on consumers. Currently stalled in committee as of June 2025.

bills.title: Generative AI Training Data transparency
bills.ai_summary: Would mandate that companies disclose what data was used to train generative AI systems in California. Currently stalled in committee as of June 2025.

bills.title: Housing Rental Algorithmic pricing regulation
bills.ai_summary: Would prohibit landlords and rental platforms in California from using algorithmic pricing to set rents dynamically. Currently stalled in committee as of June 2025.

bills.title: Workplace Surveillance using AI
bills.ai_summary: Would restrict employers in California from using AI-based surveillance tools to monitor workers without consent and disclosure. Currently stalled in committee as of June 2025.

bills.title: Mental Health and AI study
bills.ai_summary: Would establish a study in California on the impacts of AI and algorithms on mental health outcomes. Currently stalled in committee as of June 2025.

bills.title: Workplace Surveillance Tools regulation
bills.ai_summary: Would regulate workplace monitoring tools using AI in California, requiring employer disclosure and limiting invasive surveillance. Currently stalled in committee as of June 2025.

bills.title: Employment AI study
bills.ai_summary: Would create a California study examining how AI affects employment, hiring, and worker protections. Currently stalled in committee as of June 2025.

bills.title: Algorithmic Collusion Act
bills.ai_summary: Would make algorithmic collusion—where AI systems coordinate pricing or behavior illegally—subject to antitrust enforcement in California. Currently stalled in committee as of June 2025.

bills.title: Elections Communication AI disclosure
bills.ai_summary: Would require disclosure when AI-generated or AI-manipulated content is used in election communications in California. Currently stalled in committee as of June 2025.

bills.title: App Store Accountability Act - Age verification and parental consent for app downloads by minors
bills.ai_summary: Requires app stores to implement age verification and obtain parental consent before minors can download apps in Texas. Signed into law by Governor Abbott on May 27, 2025, creating new compliance requirements for digital marketplaces.

bills.title: Data Center Impact Analysis and Report
bills.ai_summary: Would have required data center operators to analyze and report environmental and community impacts in Maryland. Vetoed by the Governor on May 22, 2025 and did not become law.

bills.title: Data Center Impact Analysis and Report (crossfile of SB116)
bills.last_action: Vetoed by the Governor (veto overridden December 2025)
bills.ai_summary: Requires data center operators in Maryland to analyze and report environmental and community impacts. Vetoed by the Governor on May 22, 2025 but the veto was overridden in December 2025, making it law.

bills.title: Study resolution on data centers
bills.ai_summary: Would have directed Montana to study data center development and impacts. Died in standing committee on May 22, 2025 and did not advance.

bills.title: Establish the Montana data center development act
bills.ai_summary: Would have created a Montana data center development program and incentives. Died in the legislative process on May 20, 2025 and did not become law.

bills.title: TAKE IT DOWN Act - Nonconsensual intimate images and deepfakes federal prohibition
bills.last_action: Signed into law by President Trump
bills.ai_summary: Federally prohibits creation and distribution of nonconsensual intimate images and deepfakes, with criminal penalties. Signed into law by President Trump on May 19, 2025, establishing the first major federal prohibition on deepfake-based sexual abuse material.

bills.title: Revise taxes for class 17 data center property
bills.last_action: Assigned chapter number by Secretary of State
bills.ai_summary: Revises the tax classification and treatment of data center property in Montana to create a class 17 designation with modified tax rates. The bill has been enacted and assigned a chapter number, making it law effective immediately.

bills.title: AI in Consumer Transactions Disclosure Requirements
bills.ai_summary: Requires businesses to disclose when AI is used in consumer transactions and obtain consent before deploying AI for decision-making affecting consumers. The law is now in effect as of May 7, 2025, establishing Utah as an early adopter of AI transparency requirements.

bills.title: Protection Against Foreign Adversarial Artificial Intelligence Act of 2025
bills.ai_summary: Would prohibit the federal government from procuring, using, or funding AI systems developed or controlled by foreign adversaries, with penalties for violations. The bill remains in early committee review with no timeline for passage.

bills.title: Prohibit Federal Government from using facial recognition technology for identity verification
bills.ai_summary: Would prohibit the federal government from using facial recognition technology for identity verification purposes in any agency or program. The bill is still in committee with uncertain prospects for advancement.

bills.title: Right to Compute Act - Critical infrastructure AI risk management
bills.ai_summary: Requires critical infrastructure operators to implement AI risk management practices and conduct assessments of AI systems used in their operations. The law is now in effect in Montana, establishing baseline AI governance requirements for essential services.

bills.title: Prohibition on AI-Powered Robot Harassment and Stalking
bills.ai_summary: Criminalizes the use of AI-powered robots to harass or stalk individuals, adding penalties for automated harassment and stalking offenses. The law is now in effect in North Dakota, providing a new legal remedy against AI-enabled harassment.

bills.title: Sales tax exemption for construction or remodeling of qualified data centers with minimum $250 million investment
bills.ai_summary: Provides Kansas sales tax exemption for construction and remodeling of data centers that meet a minimum $250 million investment threshold, incentivizing major data center development. The bill was approved by the Governor on April 24, 2025, and is now law.

bills.title: Rate Payer Protection Act - Data Center Planning Commission
bills.last_action: Referred to House Committee on Rules, Calendar, and Operations
bills.ai_summary: Establishes a Data Center Planning Commission to protect North Carolina ratepayers from unexpected costs and impacts of data center development on energy infrastructure. The bill is pending review in the House Rules Committee with no immediate action expected.

bills.title: Data centers; site assessment for high energy use facility - sound profile, water and agricultural resource effects
bills.ai_summary: Would have required data centers to conduct site assessments evaluating impacts on sound profiles, water resources, and agricultural land before development. The Governor's veto kills this bill, blocking new environmental review requirements for data centers in Virginia.

bills.title: Data Center Development Act - Establishes Data Center Development Office, requires siting permits, performance-based efficiency standards
bills.ai_summary: Establishes a Data Center Development Office in South Carolina, requires siting permits for new data centers, and mandates performance-based energy efficiency standards. The bill is currently in committee review and has not yet advanced to a floor vote.

bills.title: CREATE AI Act of 2025 - Creating Resources for Every American To Experiment with AI
bills.ai_summary: Would establish federal funding and resources to expand public access to AI tools and training for consumers and small businesses. The bill remains in committee without scheduled floor action.

bills.title: AI and Critical Technology Workforce Framework Act of 2025
bills.ai_summary: Would establish a federal framework for developing and training the AI and critical technology workforce through grants and partnerships. The bill is in early committee stage with no timeline for consideration.

bills.title: Frank Broyles Publicity Rights - Protection against AI-generated likenesses
bills.ai_summary: Protects individuals' publicity rights against unauthorized use of their likeness in AI-generated content without consent or compensation. The law is now in effect in Arkansas, giving people legal recourse against unauthorized AI impersonation.

bills.title: AI-Generated Content Model Training and Ownership
bills.ai_summary: Requires AI model developers to disclose training data sources and establishes ownership rights for individuals whose content is used to train AI systems. The law is now in effect in Arkansas, creating transparency and ownership protections in AI training.

bills.title: Criminal liability for AI-generated imagery indistinguishable from real minors
bills.ai_summary: Creates criminal liability for creating, distributing, or possessing AI-generated sexual imagery that appears indistinguishable from real minors. The law is now in effect in Arkansas, establishing felony penalties for synthetic child sexual abuse material.

bills.title: Public Entity AI Policy Requirements
bills.ai_summary: Requires all public entities in Arkansas to develop and adopt formal AI policies governing procurement, use, and oversight of AI systems. The law is now in effect, establishing mandatory AI governance standards for state and local government.

bills.title: Private Right of Action for AI-generated content misuse
bills.ai_summary: Creates a private right of action allowing individuals to sue for damages when their likeness or voice is used in AI-generated content without authorization. The law is now in effect in Arkansas, enabling private lawsuits for AI-generated misuse.

bills.title: Penalties for distributing synthetic intimate content
bills.ai_summary: Establishes criminal penalties for distributing synthetic intimate imagery of real people without consent, targeting deepfake pornography. The law is now in effect in Arkansas, criminalizing non-consensual AI-generated intimate content.

bills.title: Prohibit use of AI in certain medical record contexts
bills.ai_summary: Prohibits the use of AI in certain medical record contexts, likely restricting automated decision-making in patient care or records management. The law is now in effect in Idaho, limiting AI deployment in specific healthcare settings.

bills.title: AI Policy Standards Development
bills.ai_summary: Directs Kentucky to develop comprehensive AI policy standards and best practices for government and regulated industries. The law is now in effect, establishing a mandate for statewide AI governance framework development.

bills.title: A bill to require data center operators to submit reports on data center energy and water use to States and the EPA
bills.last_action: Referred to Senate Committee on Environment and Public Works
bills.ai_summary: Would require data center operators to report energy and water consumption data to state governments and the EPA, creating transparency on resource use. The bill is pending in the Senate Environment and Public Works Committee with no immediate action scheduled.

bills.title: A bill to impose a moratorium on the construction of new data centers until legislation is enacted that safeguards the public from the dangers of artificial intelligence
bills.ai_summary: Would impose a moratorium on new data center construction until Congress passes AI safeguard legislation addressing public safety risks. The bill remains in committee with limited prospects given its restrictive approach to data center development.

bills.title: High-Risk Artificial Intelligence Developer and Deployer Act
bills.ai_summary: Would have regulated developers and deployers of high-risk AI systems through licensing, auditing, and liability requirements in Virginia. The Governor's veto blocks this comprehensive AI regulation bill, preventing new oversight mechanisms for high-risk AI deployment.

bills.title: New York State Sustainable Data Centers Act - Regulates energy consumption, requires disclosure, establishes surcharge
bills.ai_summary: Regulates data center energy consumption, requires sustainability disclosures, and establishes a surcharge mechanism for excessive energy use in New York. The bill is in committee review and represents New York's attempt to regulate data center environmental impacts.

bills.title: Mental Health Chatbot Regulation
bills.ai_summary: Establishes regulatory standards for mental health chatbots, likely including safety requirements, disclosure obligations, and limitations on clinical capabilities. The law is now in effect in Utah, creating the first state-level regulation of AI mental health applications.

bills.title: AI PLAN Act - Strategy against AI in financial crimes and misinformation
bills.ai_summary: Establishes a federal strategy to combat financial crimes and misinformation through AI oversight and coordination across agencies. Currently referred to committee with no movement toward passage.

bills.title: Leveraging AI to Streamline the Code of Federal Regulations Act of 2025
bills.ai_summary: Directs federal agencies to use AI to modernize and streamline the Code of Federal Regulations, reducing complexity and improving accessibility. Early committee stage with limited likelihood of near-term implementation.

bills.title: The Data Center Transparency Act - Prohibits local governments from entering into nondisclosure agreements regarding electricity or water usage of any entity
bills.ai_summary: Prohibits Georgia local governments from signing nondisclosure agreements about data centers' electricity and water usage, increasing transparency. Referred to committee on 3/5/25 with no further action.

bills.title: Data Center Transparency Act - To require reports on the effects of data centers on air quality and water quality, and on electricity consumption by data centers
bills.last_action: Referred to House Committee on Energy and Commerce
bills.ai_summary: Requires federal reports on data centers' effects on air quality, water quality, and electricity consumption to establish environmental impact baseline. Referred to House Energy and Commerce Committee with early-stage status.

bills.title: Advances the sunset date for the data center equipment sales and use tax exemption
bills.ai_summary: Extends Georgia's data center equipment sales tax exemption sunset date, continuing tax incentives for data center infrastructure investment. Referred to Senate Finance Committee.

bills.title: Repeals the data center equipment sales and use tax exemption
bills.ai_summary: Eliminates Georgia's data center equipment sales and use tax exemption, ending preferential tax treatment and potentially increasing state revenue. Referred to Senate Finance Committee where it competes with SB408.

bills.title: New York AI Bill of Rights - Rights and protections for automated decision systems
bills.ai_summary: Creates legal protections and rights for individuals affected by automated decision systems in New York, including transparency and appeal rights. Referred to committee with early-stage progression.

bills.title: AI Training Data Transparency Act
bills.ai_summary: Requires disclosure of datasets used to train AI systems in New York to promote transparency and accountability. Recently introduced with minimal progress toward passage.

bills.title: Artificial Intelligence Literacy Act - AI literacy in digital equity grant program
bills.ai_summary: Integrates AI literacy education into New York's digital equity grant program to improve public understanding of AI systems. Recently introduced with no committee action yet.

bills.title: AI Accountability Act - Government use and studies
bills.ai_summary: Establishes accountability measures for Georgia government use of AI, including pilot programs and studies on AI impacts. In committee as of 3/1/25.

bills.title: Reducing Emissions from Artificial Intelligence
bills.ai_summary: Addresses emissions and environmental impact from AI computing infrastructure in Massachusetts. Recently filed with no further legislative action.

bills.title: Protecting Consumers in Interactions with AI Systems
bills.ai_summary: Establishes consumer protections for interactions with AI systems in Massachusetts, including disclosure and safety requirements. Recently filed with no advancement.

bills.title: Fostering Artificial Intelligence Responsibility
bills.ai_summary: Creates accountability and responsibility framework for AI development and deployment in Massachusetts. Recently filed without subsequent committee referral.

bills.title: Fostering Artificial Intelligence Responsibility (Senate companion)
bills.ai_summary: Senate companion to H77, establishing AI responsibility requirements and accountability measures in Massachusetts. Recently filed in parallel track.

bills.title: AI and Software Tools in Healthcare Decision-Making
bills.ai_summary: Regulates use of AI and software tools in Massachusetts healthcare decision-making, requiring oversight and transparency. Recently filed with no committee action.

bills.title: Protections for Consumers Interacting with AI Chatbots
bills.ai_summary: Protects Massachusetts consumers interacting with AI chatbots through disclosure and safety requirements. Recently filed without further progression.

bills.title: Cybersecurity and Artificial Intelligence Act
bills.ai_summary: Combines cybersecurity standards with AI regulation in Massachusetts to protect systems from misuse. Recently filed in early legislative stage.

bills.title: Accountability and Transparency in AI Systems
bills.ai_summary: Mandates accountability and transparency in AI systems used in Massachusetts through disclosure and audit requirements. Recently filed without committee assignment.

bills.title: Promoting Economic Development with Emerging AI Models and Safety
bills.ai_summary: Balances economic development incentives for emerging AI models with safety guardrails in Massachusetts. Recently filed with minimal progression.

bills.title: Disclosure Requirements for Synthetic Media in Political Advertising
bills.ai_summary: Requires disclosure when synthetic media (deepfakes) appears in political advertisements in Massachusetts. Recently filed without further action.

bills.title: Regulating Provenance Regarding Artificial Intelligence
bills.ai_summary: Establishes provenance and origin tracking requirements for AI systems in Massachusetts to ensure accountability. Recently filed with no committee referral.

bills.title: Income tax credits for data centers in opportunity zones or owned by minority, women, or disabled businesses
bills.ai_summary: Creates Illinois income tax credits for data centers located in opportunity zones or owned by minority and women-owned businesses to incentivize investment. Referred to committee with no current action.

bills.title: Data centers; industrial zoning - requires localities to review and amend zoning ordinances for data centers
bills.last_action: Left in Counties, Cities and Towns Committee
bills.ai_summary: Requires Virginia localities to review zoning ordinances and remove barriers to data center development for industrial uses. Stalled in Counties, Cities and Towns Committee as of 2/11/25.

bills.title: Illinois Data Center Energy and Water Reporting Act - Requires all data centers to annually report energy and water consumption beginning January 1, 2026
bills.ai_summary: Mandates all Illinois data centers report annual energy and water consumption starting January 2026 to track environmental impact. Referred to committee on 2/5/25 without further progression.

bills.title: Revises sunset date for exemption for certain high-technology data center equipment from sales and use tax
bills.last_action: Referred to House Ways and Means Committee
bills.ai_summary: Extends Georgia's high-technology data center equipment tax exemption sunset date to continue tax incentives for data center expansion. Referred to House Ways and Means Committee.

bills.title: Electric utilities; data center cost allocation - directs SCC to determine if non-data center customers subsidize data centers
bills.ai_summary: Virginia SB960 directs the State Corporation Commission to investigate whether non-data center utility customers are subsidizing data center operations through their rates. Currently in committee review as of January 2025.

bills.title: Data centers: power usage effectiveness: cost shifts - Requires data centers to incorporate load-management and demand response capabilities
bills.ai_summary: California AB222 mandates that data centers incorporate load-management and demand response capabilities to reduce peak power consumption and grid strain. The bill was introduced and referred to committee in January 2025.

bills.title: An Act Concerning Energy And Water Efficiency Requirements For Artificial Intelligence Data Centers
bills.last_action: Referred to Joint Committee on Energy and Technology
bills.ai_summary: Connecticut HB05076 establishes energy and water efficiency requirements specifically for artificial intelligence data centers to limit their environmental impact. The bill was referred to the Joint Committee on Energy and Technology in January 2025.

bills.title: Sales and Use Tax Law: exemptions: certified data center facilities
bills.ai_summary: California SB58 proposes a sales and use tax exemption for certified data center facilities, reducing their tax burden. The bill was introduced and referred to committee in January 2025.

bills.title: AI in Employment Decisions - Amends Human Rights Act to prohibit discriminatory AI use
bills.ai_summary: Illinois HB3773 amends the Human Rights Act to prohibit employers from using AI in hiring, promotion, or employment decisions in a way that discriminates based on protected characteristics. The law became effective January 1, 2026.

bills.title: Consumer Protections for Artificial Intelligence - Algorithmic discrimination prevention
bills.last_action: Signed by Governor Polis, effective Feb 1, 2026 (delayed to June 30, 2026)
bills.ai_summary: Colorado SB24-205 establishes consumer protections against algorithmic discrimination by requiring AI systems to avoid unfair treatment based on protected characteristics. The law was signed by Governor Polis and is effective June 30, 2026 (after a delayed implementation).
